title: Screening of phytochemical, free radical scavenging activity and Total phenolic content of Raphanus caudatus pods
author: Akshat Sumra
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This study aimed to evaluate the phytochemical, Phenolic and Flavonoid analysis by using seven different solvent extraction system of Raphanus caudatus pods from Brassicaceae family. Antioxidant properties has been detected in fresh extracts when compared with dried extracts of ethanol, methanol and hexane due to the presence of sulforaphane and Sulforaphane using DPPH assay along with that total phenolic content was measured using folin- ciocalteu reagent keeping gallic acid as standard. Presence of these secondary metabolites indicates the medicinal properties of R. caudatus which is the richest bioresources further being used from plant origin which includes various groups such as tannins, flavonoids, Polysterols, essential oils, terpenoids, alkaloids, glycosides etc. extensively used for therapeutic use.

Presence of these secondary metabolites indicates the medicinal properties of R. caudatus which is the richest bioresources further being used from plant origin which includes various groups such as tannins, flavonoids, Polysterols, essential oils, terpenoids, alkaloids, glycosides etc. extensively used for therapeutic use.
keywords: DPPH assay, phytochemistry, Raphanus caudatus, Green adjuvants, Plant extracts.
